response:
High level
- Remediation in azr-compliance-v3 is a managed “ensure Event Grid subscription” workflow that can be run per‑subscription or as a bulk operation. It is implemented as a Fleet-backed flow that calls the Fleet/APIM Event Grid ensure endpoint, polls for completion, and surfaces per‑subscription status in the Event Grid Details UI.

Key pieces and flow
1. Scope / inventory
   - SyncEventGrid builds the inventory and intentionally limits remediation candidates to Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions only (join to lighthouse_delegations, managed_by_tenant_id = Rackspace Lighthouse tenant, delegation_state in active/succeeded). Event Grid rows are further filtered by naming/destination patterns (rax-/rackspace- naming, rackspace.com / compliance-v3 hosts, known Fleet resource IDs). GetEventGridDeployments exposes metadata.coverage (deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions) so the UI shows “Ready For Event Grid Deployment” vs “Monitoring Required First”.

2. Operator actions in the UI
   - The EventGridDetails page shows per‑subscription “Ensure Event Grid” and a global “Ensure All Deployable” (bulk) action and local remediation state badges.
   - The frontend issues a single POST to start remediation and then polls status until completion, merging returned item statuses into the deployable cards.

3. Per‑subscription / backend call details
   - Frontend → backend path (current flow): POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure (backend then calls APIM).
   - Backend obtains a Rackspace identity token (shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') using RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD), sends Authorization: Bearer to the APIM Event Grid ensure endpoint (https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription), and polls the returned statusUrl until a terminal runtimeStatus.
   - Polling semantics / result mapping: treat succeeded and already_exists as success; 404 → not_found; 409 → not_eligible.

4. Bulk remediation implementation
   - API endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk to start; GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest to fetch latest run and per-item statuses.
   - Persistence: Postgres tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items store runs and item statuses (so UI can refresh and show continuity).
   - Start behavior: the bulk start reuses an active queued/running run (if present) and snapshots the full deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL for that run.
   - Worker: a dedicated timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation runs every ~15s, guarded by its own advisory lock (design avoids reusing the generic sync_queue). The worker processes run items and updates the run_items table with status.
   - Client behavior: the EventGridDetails client POSTs once to start and polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges item statuses into cards and shows a “Latest Bulk Remediation” panel.

Why the backend mediation and token approach
- The flow was originally implemented as a browser-side Fleet call (because the browser has the operator MSAL token) but changed to backend mediation because Fleet requires application permission. The backend initially used Entra client credentials, then the implementation was revised to reuse the Rackspace Identity flow (RACKSPACE_USERNAME/PASSWORD) to acquire a token sent to APIM.

Summary
- Remediation is a scoped, Fleet/APIM‑backed ensure operation (single or bulk) that the azr-compliance-v3 frontend triggers and polls. Inventory scoping ensures only Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions are targeted. Bulk runs are persisted in Postgres and processed by a dedicated 15s worker so the UI can show live per‑subscription status and recover across page refreshes.

The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation; credential_type: ClientSecretCredential; token_scope: api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default; apim_subscription_endpoint: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription; frontend_request: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure; reason_for_backend_flow: Fleet requires application permission; backend_behavior: polls returned statusUrl until completion; previous_flow: browser-side Fleet API call]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, and 'Monitoring Required First' for subscriptions outside monitoring scope. This resolves ambiguity in the prior 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' card, which only reflected monitoring-entitled scope.

Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.

SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscript
